curves.interpretation: Approximate flat anchor converted from average -180 dB PSD relative to 1 (m s^-2)^2 Hz^-1.
curves.model_uncertainty_note: The paper reports an average level and frequency-dependent resonances; flat interpolation is an atlas assumption, not a digitized trace.
curves.operating_conditions: Three-channel-correlation self-noise estimate for quiet co-located iGrav instruments at J9 Strasbourg; environmental station noise is not represented.
curves.source_reported_quantity: approximately -180 dB average PSD from 1e-3 to 1e-1 Hz for iGrav015/iGrav032 and comparison instruments at J9

curves.interpretation: Flat short-term sensitivity anchor; upper edge follows reported servo-loop response change near 0.05 Hz.
curves.model_uncertainty_note: The 1e-3 Hz lower edge is an explicit atlas modelling choice, not a paper-stated white-noise boundary; long-term coloured noise/stability must be modelled separately.
curves.operating_conditions: AQG-A01 field measurements; reported short-term sensitivity 750 nm s^-2 Hz^-1/2.
curves.source_reported_quantity: 750 nm s^-2 Hz^-1/2; long-term stability below 10 nm s^-2; servo-loop response decreases above about 0.05 Hz

curves.interpretation: Flat short-term sensitivity anchor ending at the Nyquist frequency implied by the reported 36 s effective sampling interval.
curves.model_uncertainty_note: The 1e-3 Hz lower edge is an atlas choice; drop-to-drop sampling, dead time, site vibration, and long-term systematics are omitted.
curves.operating_conditions: FG5#228 comparison at Larzac; reported short-term sensitivity 450 nm s^-2 Hz^-1/2 and effective sampling interval 36 s.
curves.source_reported_quantity: 450 nm s^-2 Hz^-1/2 with 36 s effective sampling interval

curves.interpretation: Flat illustrative anchor at the paper-reported 4 E Hz^-1/2 sensitivity.
curves.model_uncertainty_note: The frequency interval is an atlas placeholder because the abstract reports sensitivity but not a white-noise band; do not use outside sensitivity analysis without full-paper response extraction.
curves.operating_conditions: Differential light-pulse atom interferometer with 10 m accelerometer separation.
curves.source_reported_quantity: 4 E Hz^-1/2 gravity-gradient sensitivity; better than 1 E accuracy

curves.interpretation: Conservative upper end of the reported 1-5 mE Hz^-1/2 mission noise-density target.
curves.model_uncertainty_note: Spaceborne GOCE design/performance context is not a ground instrument and must be plotted as a separate category.
curves.operating_conditions: GOCE electrostatic gravity gradiometer design at approximately 250 km altitude.
